divergence: A plague wiped out 90% of humanity in 2020
story_angle: propaganda triumph narrated with unsettling sincerity
headline: Synthetic Utopias Rise from the Ashes: A Neon Dreamscape in 1986
deck: New malls resurrect the spirit of consumerism, offering pixelated escapes from the haunting echoes of a past that almost was.
article:
In a world where the shadows of 2020’s devastating plague linger like last year's fashion, humanity has discovered refuge in the glowing embrace of synthetic utopias. Across the landscape, shiny new malls punctuate the skyline, constructed from the abandoned shells of cities that once thrived. These monuments to excess beckon citizens with promises of pastel experiences, their hallways resonating with synthesized beats reminiscent of a world that reveled in frivolity.

Amidst the neon glow, virtual reality escapism is marketed as the ultimate remedy for collective trauma. Companies like DreamWave Inc. present their VR experiences as therapeutic marvels, claiming to heal emotional scars with pixelated paradises. "Why dwell on the past when you can surf through streams of nostalgia?" croons the latest ad spot, splashed across glossy billboards. The absurdity of it all delights, as weary souls plug in to experience a faux world, where the only danger is running out of battery life.

As shopping carts roll through these artificial landscapes, the pastel colors and retro aesthetics plastered everywhere serve as a sardonic reminder of our collective penchant for denial. Vendors peddle garish merchandise claiming to revive simpler pleasures—plastic toys with retro designs that promise a return to an era never truly lived. A shopper wandering through the newly opened HyperMall in suburban Neo-Atlanta remarked, "It’s like shopping in a time capsule that forgot to age. It's comforting in a completely unsettling way."

But beneath the glossy veneer lies an irony too rich to overlook: the more we cling to these synthetic delights, the more we reveal our desperation. As humanity binds itself in neon chains, seeking solace in the absurd, the specter of 2020 fades further into the background—only to be replaced by an insatiable hunger for distraction. In this new age, perhaps the greatest triumph is not merely surviving, but mastering the art of forgetting through endless consumption. Embrace the irony, dear reader, for this is our brave new world shaped by vapid desires and pixelated fantasies.
author: Local Editor

oped.title: Neon Graves: Vaporwave's Mockery of the Missing
oped.author: Morgan Voss
oped.body: While 1986's elites peddle synthetic utopias in pastel neon, they dance on the future graves of 90% wiped out by 2020's plague. Vaporwave isn't escape—it's the soundtrack of denial, a retro filter sanitizing mass death into chic consumerism. We aren't rising from ashes; we're embalming the lost in synth beats. This dreamscape is our final, empty product.

cf Step into the vaporwave universe with our exclusive line of pastel-colored activewear! Perfect for those leisure moments in the new utopia. Each piece crafted to fit comfortably and inspire freedom of expression in this transformed society. Order today and redefine your style! cf

cf Embrace the retro-futuristic revolution! Get your hands on vintage tech gadgets reimagined for the modern vaporwave enthusiast. From neon cassette players to glitch-inspired gaming consoles. Bring the 80s back in style! Limited availability—don't miss out! cf

panels.caption: The new atrium just added twelve more Roman busts blasting 'Take On Me'.
The developer leans in: 'We paved the old district for the food court. Twice the square footage, zero complaints.'
A shopper nods at the empty escalator: 'Perfect. More room for the ghosts to window-shop.'

joke.setup: In 1986 the billboards already promise neon utopias built from the ashes of 2020,
joke.punchline: because nothing moves product like pre-selling luxury sarcophagi to a species that hasn’t finished dying yet.
joke.text: In 1986 the billboards already promise neon utopias built from the ashes of 2020, because nothing moves product like pre-selling luxury sarcophagi to a species that hasn’t finished dying yet.

ads.headline: Escape to the Neon Abyss!
ads.body: Experience the tranquility of virtual beaches and pixelated sunsets with our state-of-the-art Synthwave VR headset. Immerse yourself in a world where time stands still and the only limit is your imagination.

ads.headline: Reclaim Your Dreams with Mood Oasis!
ads.body: Transform your living space into a serene escape with our Mood Oasis light projectors. Create a personal utopia filled with soothing colors and vibes, perfect for those quiet evenings in your post-plague sanctuary.

ads.headline: Revive the Past: Retro Rewind Records!
ads.body: Rediscover the magic of music with our curated selection of vinyl records from before the great collapse. Each album is a portal to a bygone era, designed to rekindle your soul and elevate your listening experience.
